claims.claim_1: CLAIM 1: 66.5% of AP+ labels are contaminated (ongoing anomaly in prediction horizon)
claims.claim_2: CLAIM 2: LR (0-training) beats oracle on genuine AP (strict): 0.703 vs 0.648 (+0.055, p=0.0000)
claims.claim_4: CLAIM 4: 97.9% of strict AP+ events are onset windows for regular anomaly blocks (100-step blocks)
claims.claim_5: CLAIM 5: Contamination creates 0.168 AUROC swing: Oracle appears +0.113 better (standard AP) but is actually -0.055 worse (strict AP)
claims.claim_6: CLAIM 6: F1-tol inflation 8.1x: raw F1=5.35% -> F1-tol=43.1%; random scores achieve 68.1% F1-tol > A2P's 67.55%
claims.claim_7: CLAIM 7: SMD oracle AUROC = 0.346 (sub-random!) with all 38 channels; top-5 channels = 0.704
claims.claim_8: CLAIM 8: LR (4 variance features, no training) beats A2P by +0.108 AUROC (10.8%)
claims.claim_9: CLAIM 9: Practical AUROC ceiling (oracle ensemble, type-optimal routing) = 0.655; not the headline 0.745
claims.claim_10: CLAIM 10: 13.5% of AP+ (Type D) are genuinely unpredictable from 200-step context windows
